mission.title: n8n: AWS (Assume Role) Credential Not Working in Workflows
mission.description: AWS (Assume Role) Credential Not Working in Workflows. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify n8n troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your n8n deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n -n n8n --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review n8n configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant n8n configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get configmap -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=n8n
```
### Bug Description

This is in relation to the new AWS (Assume Role) Credential introduced in this PR and became available in 1.119.0:

https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/20626

steps.title: Apply the fix for AWS (Assume Role) Credential Not Working in Workflows
steps.description:
can you please help prioritize this internally? As it stands, this credential is not working at all with system credential role assumption and has instance-level security considerations as long as it is not fixed. #22859 at a minimum should fix the major problems described in this issue.

EDIT: as
```yaml
[ ] I have updated to the latest stable version of n8n [please specify version number] and I am still experiencing this issue
[ ] Please close this issue – I am no longer experiencing it
[ ] Please close this issue – [please specify a reason]
```

steps.title: Confirm AWS (Assume Role) Credential Not Working in… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n -n n8n --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n n8n --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: can you please help prioritize this internally? As it stands, this credential is not working at all with system credential role assumption and has instance-level security considerations as long as it is not fixed. #22859 at a minimum should fix the major problems described in this issue.
codeSnippets:
[ ] I have updated to the latest stable version of n8n [please specify version number] and I am still experiencing this issue
[ ] Please close this issue – I am no longer experiencing it
[ ] Please close this issue – [please specify a reason]

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with n8n installed or the issue environment reproducible.
